items.notes: Synthetic interval 7,500-10,000 for most modern vehicles. 5K is conservative/safe.
Replace if visibly dirty; typical life 15K-30K
Replace if musty smell or reduced airflow; typical life 15K-20K
Replace every 2-3 years or if dark/contaminated
Check level and condition; some vehicles specify 50K-100K intervals

items.service: Tire condition and tread depth check

items.notes: Iridium/platinum plugs may go to 100K; check owner's manual
Coolant degrades and becomes acidic over time
Drain and fill, NOT flush. 'Lifetime' fluid often means lifetime of a lease, not the vehicle.
Replace if cracked, frayed, or glazed
If interference engine — failure can destroy the engine. Timing chains have no scheduled interval.
Often replaced with timing belt since labor overlaps
All fluids should be inspected and serviced

common_upsells.notes: Rarely needed on modern vehicles running quality fuel. Legitimate only if you have specific driveability symptoms.
Can cause harm on high-mileage engines by dislodging deposits that were acting as seals. Occasionally useful on genuinely neglected engines.
A drain-and-fill is sufficient and safer. A full flush can cause problems on older or high-mileage transmissions.

common_upsells.service: Cabin air filter at every visit
common_upsells.notes: Lasts 15K-20K miles. Usually behind the glove box — easy to check and replace yourself.
Only relevant for hydraulic power steering. Electric power steering systems have no fluid to flush.

common_upsells.service: Coolant flush at every oil change
common_upsells.notes: Most vehicles need coolant service every 50K-100K miles, not at every oil change.
